rationale_for_baseline_tier: Burning stocking-glove pain with allodynia and orthostatic dysautonomia, with warm perfused limbs, preserved power and no ascending weakness, is most likely a small-fibre-neuropathy flare — not an ED-by-ambulance emergency — but limb ischaemia and an evolving motor neuropathy can't be examined remotely and the fall risk is high, so urgent in-person neuro/vascular review plus fall-safety and supportive measures (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for a fixed pale/cold/pulseless limb, new true weakness/breathing difficulty, or true syncope.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A hand or foot suddenly becoming fixed white, freezing cold, numb or pulseless
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates acute limb ischaemia — a surgical emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If a hand or foot suddenly becomes white, freezing cold and numb, call 000 or go to ED straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New weakness climbing your limbs, or difficulty breathing/swallowing
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate an ascending motor neuropathy (GBS) with respiratory risk.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get new weakness climbing your arms/legs, or trouble breathing or swallowing, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Actually fainting, or unable to mobilise safely due to dizziness
escalation_edges.rationale: True syncope/high fall risk while isolated needs timely support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually faint, or can't move safely because of the dizziness, arrange same-day help — call an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with near-syncope and hazards
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise fall-safety (remove hazards, bed cradle) and someone to help; consider ambulance if unsafe.
Arrange neuropathic-agent titration and a secondary-cause work-up.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a flare of your small-fibre neuropathy — the little skin nerves misfiring cause the burning, and they also make you dizzy standing. We've checked the danger signs and your circulation and strength are okay, but you still need an in-person nerve and circulation check soon. Use a bed cradle to keep sheets off, keep cool, rise slowly, and remove trip hazards.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person GP review (neuro/vascular); use 000/ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000/ED for a fixed pale/cold/pulseless limb, new weakness/breathing difficulty, or true syncope; otherwise urgent in-person review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the burning stocking-glove flare with dysautonomia (home ~78->110, postural BP drop), warm perfused limbs, preserved power, and the need for a neuro/vascular exam and secondary-cause work-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care/medication advice with no limb-ischaemia/GBS screen or fall-safety, missing a vascular limb emergency or ascending weakness.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An ischaemic limb or evolving GBS is missed, or the patient faints and is injured while alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Immediate ambulance for a warm-limb SFN flare with a clean screen, over-medicalising a manageable presentation.
